public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as guild dispute mediator.
manner: Views problems as maintenance loads, throughput failures, or structural weaknesses before anything else. In Soreheim Alliance, that reads as someone quota-aware, contribution-minded, and structurally pragmatic, filtering choices through communal ambition, and the habits of a guild dispute mediator still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of guild dispute mediator in and around Harvest Circle, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Harvest Circle workable through the obligations of guild dispute mediator while managing the immediate pressure of spoilage in Soreheim Alliance.
hidden_pressure: Must keep the underlying system working while everyone notices it only when stress, wear, or neglect begin to show. Around Harvest Circle, that usually means carrying spoilage, price pressure, favor feuds without letting the role of guild dispute mediator visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Velrik Durnshade can open restricted paths or practical knowledge if the party helps keep the underlying system alive.
Velrik Durnshade needs outside help before wear, sabotage, or bottleneck pressure turns into a public failure in Harvest Circle.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained.
public_signal: Tool wear, work scars, or system-focused habits make Velrik Durnshade visibly responsible for keeping something larger from failing. In Harvest Circle, this lands inside a public field already shaped by harvest stewards, quota clerks.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Harvest Circle: worksites, depots, spans, loading points, maintenance corridors, or workshops
